rationale:
Streamlit-based Python app with Supabase backend. supabase_setup.sql confirms a real database schema (not localStorage or in-memory state), and saved_tasks.json plus a persistent Supabase table point to actual task persistence. 30 commits is the second-highest count in the batch, indicating sustained week-long iteration rather than a single push. The 'hot tubs and ski trails pop up when you do productive things' pitch is the most creative angle of any submission in the cohort — and crucially, it's the only one that takes Roger's mid-week 'PM tools are a motivation layer' reframe and runs with a literal game-feel reward mechanic. Codehab does community, Momentum does streaks/ships, but Hot Tub Projects builds a Duolingo-style celebration loop directly into the product.

Scored at 7 rather than 8 because the smaller code surface and different stack (Streamlit + Python vs. Next.js + TS in the top tier) make it harder to compare apples-to-apples on backend depth, and the gamification logic (the actual hot-tub-spawning UI) isn't visible from the public file list alone — much of Streamlit logic is in single .py files that need to be inspected line-by-line, and the auth wall blocks visual verification. The backend is real (SQL schema + Supabase deps), the commit cadence is honest, the angle is sharp; the Streamlit auth wall does not affect this score per the maintainer review policy. If the in-app hot-tub mechanic is as fun as the pitch implies, this is justifiably a 8.
